title: translation of human
wikitext:
[]: translation of "human"             Quote Post by nomis1242 » Sat Nov 13, 2010 5:58 pm I'm wondering if the ambiguous use of the word "human" (see wiki ) is due to dodgy translations. Perhaps it was meant that Adam/angels/evas are "beings" and the translation to "human" is misleading? Is there any other reference to Adam (and therefore the angels) being a descendant of Lilith, aside from Misato in Rebirth? This is at odds with the black/white moon theory, do you think that she's misinformed?

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sat Nov 13, 2010 6:08 pm I don't know about the "human" thing, but Adam being a descendant of Lilith was indeed a translation mistake. Adam and Lilith are/were two separate progenitors of life, not meant to coexist on the same planet. The Angels came from Adam, and the Lilim came from Lilith. Considering that Adam, Lilith, and the Lilim are for whatever the hell Anno reason considered to be a part of the same list of Angel numbering that the actual Angels are, it's no wonder it was so easily confused in translation.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Nov 13, 2010 6:14 pm it really depends on if Misato used word ningen or hito when calling Angels "alternative paths" of humanity and that humanity was 18th Angel. Can't remember it myself

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Sat Nov 13, 2010 9:30 pm Xard wrote: it really depends on if Misato used word ningen or hito

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Nov 13, 2010 10:47 pm Baz wrote: So there you have it. Shinrui is the word. You guys can debate the finer points of translation.

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Sat Nov 13, 2010 10:49 pm Azathoth wrote: I think it's read "jinrui", though.

[]:             Quote Post by nomis1242 » Sun Nov 14, 2010 3:41 am Cool, thanks everyone for the translation help. From what I can tell the black/white moon theory is commonly accepted, yeah? So we should discard Misato's statement about angels being another possibility of human evolution?

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Sun Nov 14, 2010 8:53 am nomis1242 wrote: So we should discard Misato's statement about angels being another possibility of human evolution?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Nov 14, 2010 12:35 pm Angels and Lilin (us) are both humans, they're just different kinds of humans. This is a fact regardless of what Misato actually said.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sun Nov 14, 2010 12:52 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Angels and Lilin (us) are both humans, they're just different kinds of humans. This is a fact regardless of what Misato actually said.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Nov 15, 2010 3:34 pm But what brings them all together, though, is that despite their physical bodies, they all have the same type of souls as sentient entities. Shinji and the others are still considered human even during Instrumentality, when they have no bodies of any kind.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Nov 15, 2010 3:41 pm AuraTwilight wrote: But what brings them all together, though, is that despite their physical bodies, they all have the same type of souls as sentient entities.

[]:             Quote Post by Cody MacArthur Fett » Tue Nov 16, 2010 12:31 am AuraTwilight wrote: Angels and Lilin (us) are both humans, they're just different kinds of humans. This is a fact regardless of what Misato actually said.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Nov 16, 2010 12:46 am Cody MacArthur Fett wrote: It makes absolutely no sense, but it does provide the interesting bit of canon that a Lilin like Kensuke or Maya need only believe that they are a different to change their form -- if that doesn't show up some time in the next decade in Eva I'd be truly surprised.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Nov 16, 2010 12:51 am Cody MacArthur Fett wrote: It's interesting to note though that while the Angels (including the Lilin) are couldn't be farer from "human" biology-wise that etymology-wise using the term human to describe them is perfectly correct. This is because human comes from a Greek word meaning "of the earth," and since both the Black and White Moons from which the Angels are spawned landed on Earth and did their business there calling them humans would technically be correct.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Tue Nov 16, 2010 1:23 am In reality there's actually still a bit of a bit of mystery regarding what exactly causes our cells to maintain shape and not simply dissolve. We are made of some minerals, chemicals and lots of water, so if for some reason whatever is holding us together stopped doing its thing, all that would be left of us is minerals, chemicals and water. For that matter, we're not even 100% sure what prevents the atoms in our molecules from going nuclear at any given moment. It probably involves a bunch of quantum physics and other crap that I'm too lazy and stupid to bother studying, because at the end of the day dissecting the show's jargon doesn't really affect my appreciation or enjoyment of Evangelion one way or another.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Nov 16, 2010 1:27 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: Are you sure that it comes from Greek? I thought that the English adjective "Human" and noun "Man" ultimately come from Latin through Old French.

[]:             Quote Post by beaverteeth92 » Tue Nov 16, 2010 1:59 am Timstuff wrote: In reality there's actually still a bit of a bit of mystery regarding what exactly causes our cells to maintain shape and not simply dissolve. We are made of some minerals, chemicals and lots of water, so if for some reason whatever is holding us together stopped doing its thing, all that would be left of us is minerals, chemicals and water. For that matter, we're not even 100% sure what prevents the atoms in our molecules from going nuclear at any given moment. It probably involves a bunch of quantum physics and other crap that I'm too lazy and stupid to bother studying, because at the end of the day dissecting the show's jargon doesn't really affect my appreciation or enjoyment of Evangelion one way or another.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Nov 16, 2010 3:25 pm Souls don't seem to be absolutely limited to sentient beings in Eva, though. And if ultimately the difference between human and angel goes beyond simply the different forms they take, down to the soul itself...is there a difference between Adam's Chamber of Gauf and Lilith's? What is that difference?

[]:             Quote Post by nomis1242 » Tue Nov 16, 2010 5:12 pm Baz wrote: We don't have to, because Misato never said that. She said "angels are another possibility for humans (jinrui)" which is much more vague. She never used the word "evolution."
